title: Scientists Reverse Alzheimer's Through the Brain's Cleanup Door - TCR 05/18/26
description:
An IBEC and West China Hospital team reversed Alzheimer's-like pathology in aged mice by restoring the blood-brain barrier's LRP1 amyloid-clearance machinery with supramolecular nanoparticles, Cambridge published a Nature paper wiring lanthanide nanoparticles into the first working near-infrared LED at five volts with 98 percent energy transfer, and Britain installed 27,607 solar arrays in March to cross two million rooftop installations as Iran-war fuel prices flipped household energy math.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-18-2026/

0:00 UK crosses two million solar rooftops
1:47 Alzheimer's reversed through the brain's cleanup door
4:47 Cambridge wires the 40-year-insulating LED class
7:41 AI chip stack absorbs the Gulf disruption
11:44 Two Million Roofs
14:15 Close

This episode covers the IBEC and West China Hospital Sichuan University blood-brain barrier therapy, the Cavendish Laboratory triplet-transfer near-infrared LED breakthrough, the Washington University platinum-free electrolyzer cathode that ran 1,000 hours at industrial current density, the UK crossing two million solar installations, Queen Mary's seven-day fasting proteomic map across 3,000 blood proteins, and Q1 earnings from TSMC, Samsung, and SK Hynix absorbing the eleven-week Gulf helium and LNG disruption into AI compute pricing.

For fifteen years, Alzheimer's therapy has run on antibodies that bind amyloid plaque. A Cochrane review two weeks ago found no clinically meaningful benefit across twenty thousand patients. The IBEC and West China Hospital Sichuan University team went a different direction: restoring the LRP1 transport protein that, in a healthy brain, clears amyloid-β across the blood-brain barrier. Supramolecular nanoparticles mimicking LRP1's natural ligands dropped brain amyloid 50 to 60 percent in an hour, and three-dose treatments left aged mice behaving normally six months later. The Cambridge Cavendish Lab solved a forty-year photonics problem by attaching an organic antenna dye to insulating lanthanide nanoparticles, funneling charge into a triplet state at over 98 percent transfer efficiency and producing the first working near-infrared LED in this class - opening a combinatorial space of injectable medical light sources, deep-tissue photodynamic therapy, and spectrally pure optical communications. Washington University's rhenium-molybdenum phosphide electrolyzer cathode held a thousand hours at industrial current densities, closing one of the structural cost gaps that kept green hydrogen tethered to platinum. And the AI compute stack itself - TSMC up 35 percent in revenue, Samsung's operating profit up more than eightfold, SK Hynix shares up roughly 200 percent on the year - now shows the physical substrate underneath intelligence visibly contested, with the Tata-ASML India fab partnership announced as the first move in the substrate diversification the easy single-geography route had deferred.
